rulings.Case Title: YING-TSUN SU VS. NATASHA V. ALEXEEVA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 6, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANTS STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY AND WENDY PHILLIPS' MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT is DENIED. Moving party failed to cite any authority for the proposition that a defendant that has not formally appeared in the action is entitled to notice or is required to be a party to any stipulations filed in the matter. The Court notes that there was no reason for plaintiff's counsel to not reach out to the known counsel for the defendant prior to seeking leave to file an amended complaint. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: YING-TSUN SU VS. NATASHA V. ALEXEEVA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 6, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANTS STATE FARM GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY AND WENDY PHILLIPS' DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained as to all causes of action currently asserted in the complaint for the moving party to allege fact showing that coverage as described by the plaintiff was requested and defendants promised such coverage. The demurrer is otherwise overruled. Claims asserted in the First Amended Complaint relate back to the original complaint that asserted a cause of action for negligence, which the moving defendants agree is the gravamen of plaintiff's claim. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: YING-TSUN SU VS. NATASHA V. ALEXEEVA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion By Defendant Ying-Tsun Su ("Su") Under Code Of Civil Procedure Section 877.6 For Order Confirming That Settlement Was Made In Good Faith And For Order Striking All Cross-Complaints Against Su
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 6, 2024 line 1. NOTICE OF MOTION BY DEFENDANT YING-TSUN SU ("SU") UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 877.6 FOR ORDER CONFIRMING THAT SETTLEMENT WAS MADE IN GOOD FAITH AND FOR ORDER STRIKING ALL CROSS-COMPLAINTS AGAINST SU is DENIED without prejudice. Moving party failed to set out facts or argument providing the Court with sufficient information to determine the potential recovery or the settling defendant's proportional liability. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: MEGAN HARDY ET AL VS. ARON BOHLIG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 6, 2024 line 2. DEFENDANT CAWC WATERPROOFING AND RESTORATION'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer to the cause of action for negligence is overruled. This cause of action is sufficiently stated. Demurrer to the cause of action for tenant harassment is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff failed to allege any facts for the proposition that the moving defendant for the "contractor of the landlord" within the meaning of SFRO and failed to suggest any potential amendment connecting the contractor to the landlord. Demurrer to the cause of action for Negligent Infliction of emotional distress is sustained without leave to amend as neither the specific argument advanced in the demurrer, nor the authorities cited therein are mentioned in the opposition. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA VS. CRAIG GOMEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiffs Demand For Docs, Special Interrogatories And Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 6, 2024 line 10. PLAINTIFF MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S DEMAND FOR DOCS, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND FORM INTERROGATORIES is GRANTED, subject to opposition.
